content.text:
There are **two horses** in this story:

1. **One One** – the first race horse
2. **Two Two** – the second race horse (the "one too" means Two Two was also a race horse)

The rest of the tongue-twister is just playing with the sounds of "one," "won," "two," and "too":
- "One One won one race" = One One won a single race
- "Two Two won one too" = Two Two also won a single race

So both horses each won one race! 🐎🐎
